what_it_does: This file provides CSS for two UI surfaces: a source-intake panel (affiliation grid, connector chooser, captured items list) and a connector-authorization wizard modal (steps, scopes, spinner, connection list).
observations: Warm gold/bronze color palette using CSS custom properties
BEM-like prefixes: si__ for source-intake and conn- for connector modal
Grid collapses from 4 columns to 2 columns under 1100px
Recording dot and authorization spinner use keyframe animations
Captured items support thumbnails, glyphs, read toggles, and remove buttons
Only one media query and no reduced-motion handling
standouts: Strong thematic split between si__ and conn- naming
Heavy use of translucent warm backgrounds and accent-gold tokens
Recording-dot pulse animation and authorization spinner
Captured-item read toggle and remove button micro-interactions
conventions: CSS custom properties for colors, radii, easing, and status tokens
Responsive layout via a single breakpoint media query
concerns: .si__aff.active uses status-error colors, which may conflate active and error semantics
.conn-overlay references an undefined voice-fade animation name
.conn-connected is declared with only display: flex and no further rules
Very small text sizes (10px-11px) may risk readability/contrast
notes: A warm-themed stylesheet for source-intake cards and a connector-authorization modal, relying on CSS variables and micro-interactions.
questions: What do the si and conn abbreviations precisely stand for in the product domain?
Where are the CSS custom properties such as --accent-gold and --status-error defined?
Is .si__aff.active intentionally styled with error colors for selection?
How is the wizard step state and connector authorization flow driven?
apparent_themes: Tactile feedback through hover, lift, and shadow
intention_signals: It appears designed to make selecting data sources and authorizing connectors feel premium, trustworthy, and guided through a cohesive gold/bronze visual system.
novelty: A warm, paper-like translucent panel style paired with a recording-pulse animation inside a source-intake widget.
contribution: A self-contained set of presentational rules for source-intake and connector-wizard UI surfaces, including layout primitives, interactive states, and modal styling, that a fused system could reuse or remap to its token system.
summary_for_embedding: This CSS file defines layout, visual styling, and interaction states for a source-intake widget and a connector-authorization modal in a warm gold/bronze theme.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a polished, step-by-step UI for selecting sources and authorizing third-party connectors.
